text: memory_orient and memory_attention need input-level authorization, not output filtering — aggregate tools derive from multiple sources
memory_query canonical injection (meta/reference-index, people/magnus/profile, meta/conventions) must be filtered through access checks
Draft and spec disagree on denial payloads (agent denial shape, orient conventions for non-owners, memory_insights behavior)
memory_read not-found path leaks sibling keys via hint — denied access must use a separate clean path
Pattern validation must reject ambiguous patterns like users/sara* — require exact, /* suffix, or lone *
Per-tool namespace scope normalization needed — memory_history and memory_query interpret namespace parameters differently (subtree vs exact)
Shared-namespace delete incompatible with schema — entries have no principal ownership column, executeDelete wipes all entries
Non-owner namespace creation (writing to users/sara/inbox/msg1) needs explicit test in Phase 1
Delete token must be bound to principal — prevent cross-principal reuse
OAuth client_id to principal binding is unresolved — add oauth_client_id column to principals
Agent service tokens should be explicitly in or out of Phase 1 scope — current plan is ambiguous
Test matrix gaps — 13 specific missing scenarios identified including orient derived fields, history subtree, query explain mode, mixed batches
New tool default-allows without enforcement — tests only help if someone writes them. Need structural guard.
Compact conventions helper references meta/conventions which is owner-only — would disclose namespace to non-owners
Shared-namespace single-entry delete by any writer is still unsafe without entry ownership — must be all-deletes-owner-only OR add principal column

Agent service-token auth flow under-specified — need explicit AuthInfo.clientId semantics, revocation/expiry enforcement point, and whether verification is in verifyAccessToken or resolveAccessContext
Namespace canonicalization still missing — raw inputs not normalized before matching despite validator accepting slash-heavy variants
Spec must be updated to match revised design — source of truth is internally inconsistent
